formality.description: Modern Greek has a T-V distinction: εσείς (eseis, formal/plural) vs εσύ (esi, informal/singular). Professional software uses εσείς. Greek uses Δημοτική (Dimotiki, demotic/modern standard) — Καθαρεύουσα (Katharevousa, purist) is archaic.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Greek has three grammatical genders (masculine, feminine, neuter). When gender is unknown, masculine plural is the conventional default.

formal.label: Εσείς (Formal)
formal.description: Standard for professional software and business communication.
formal.prompt: Formal Modern Greek (Δημοτική). Use εσείς for second-person address. Professional register. Modern standard Greek — not Καθαρεύουσα.

informal.label: Εσύ (Informal)
informal.description: For consumer apps, social media, and casual contexts.
informal.prompt: Informal Modern Greek. Use εσύ-form. Conversational tone for consumer products.
